item.title: Graffiti needs cleaning, 4th April
item.description:
The Station Square entrance to Dunstonian Court has to bits of graffiti that need cleaning. On No.32 is on the pillar near the entrance. No. 26 is set further back along the wall underneath the rear side window. Please clean the graffiti.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Station Square, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Anti pigeon spikes hanging from railway bridge, 4th April
item.description:
There are some anti-pigeon spikes hanging down from the railway bridge, they’re dangling over the pavement by the infant school and I’m worried they might fall on someone as they walk under

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, needs cleaning Bluston Parade railway footbridge, 4th April
item.description:
Please clean these 5 incidences of graffiti from the footbridge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping from a car, 4th April
item.description:
A car pulled up while I was parked outside the station. The passenger door opened and threw out a large brown bag. The driver then turned the car around and drive off.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16, Lower Station Road, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Both, 4th April
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Unit 2, Loampit Vale, Ladywell, Greater London

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Overflowing litter bin, 4th April

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18-20, Southwark Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bike blocking pavement AGAIN, 4th April
item.description:
Can you please get this regular user to park their bike in a considerate way NOT blicking the pavement. This us a busy route for pedestrians to the tube station, buggys, disabled, families, very hard for all of us to get by. It's getting annoying now as it's regular. This isn't what your new contract in haringey expects.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing council bin - this keeps happening weekly, 4th April
item.description:
Hi there, Please sort this out. This bin meant to be collected daily and this has not been happening for a while.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dupree Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish near bushes and rats running around on path, 4th April
item.description:
There was rubbish located underneath the bushes, and I was exiting the Broadway Shopping Centre and was about to head down the path going past the Magistrates Court when I saw rubbish underneath the shrubs opposite the Court and a large rat ran from beneath the bushes onto the staircase of the Magistrates Court. I then decided to take a different route to walk. Please ensure this area is cleaned and the issue is dealt with appropriately as rats pose a serious health hazard to pedestrians due to the diseases they carry.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broadway, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots at traffic lights at crossroads, 4th April
item.description:
Holes have been there for months. Getting bigger by the day. Cannot avoid them....ruined my front tyre on Saturday 28th March....sort it out....
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southend Lane, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish on sidewalk and curb, 4th April
item.description:
Bin men mishandling the hi. Collection means rubbish accumulation on the sidewalk and the curbside.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thanet Road, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Please remove.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Denmark Hill, Camberwell, Greater London
